zh.lifeSummary: 當Guyomarch II de Léon於大約1042年出生在法國布列塔尼分尼斯艾沙吉波爾-德-萊時，他的父親Alain de Léon I24歲，母親Adelice du Quemenet26歲。他和Orwen de Cornouaille至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1103年在法國洛萊姆斯巴勒迪克享年62歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Guyomarch II de Léon于大约1042年出生在法国布列塔尼分尼斯艾沙吉波爾-德-萊时，他的父亲Alain de Léon I24岁，母亲Adelice du Quemenet26岁。他和Orwen de Cornouaille至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1103年在法国洛萊姆斯巴勒迪克享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Léon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Pedro, Jesus, Jorge, Francisco, Rafael, Miguel, Raul.

Spanish (León): habitational name from León, a city in northwestern Spain, named with Latin legio, genitive legionis ‘legion’, a division of the Roman army. In Roman times the city was the garrison of the 7th Legion, known as the Legio Gemina. The city's name became shortened from Legion(em) to Leon(em), and in this form developed an unetymological association with the word for ‘lion’ (Spanish león).

French (Léon) and Spanish (León): from the personal name Léon, León, from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn. The name Leo(n) was borne by numerous early Christian martyrs and thirteen popes.

variants.name: Len
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Zbigniew, Jozef, Karol.
Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a flax grower or merchant, from Polish len ‘flax’.
Polish (Leń): nickname from leń ‘lazybones’.

variants.name: Leonides
meanings: Hispanic (Leónides; Mexico): from the personal name Leónides, from Greek Leonidēs, itself most likely a patronymic derivative of Leōn (from leōn ‘lion’; see Leon ) + the suffix -(e)ides.

variants.name: Lyon
meanings: Scottish, English, and French: from the personal name Middle English Lyon, Old French Leon (from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn; see Leon ). Compare Lyall .
Scottish and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lyons-la-Forêt in Eure, Normandy. It is unlikely to be from the better-known southern French city of Lyon (see 5 below).

English and French: nickname from Middle English lioun ‘lion’ (Old English, Latin leo), Old French leon, perhaps applied to a brave, fierce, or proud person, or one with a shaggy mane of hair. Compare Lion .

variants.name: Leos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Pedro, Francisco, Manuel, Ruben, Carlos, Juan, Santos, Jesus, Pablo, Raul, Alfonso.
Hispanic (mainly Mexico): altered form of Basque Leoz, itself a Castilianized form (also Léoz) of a habitational name from Leotz (in Castilian: Leoz) in Navarre.
Greek: from the personal name Leos, a pet form of Leon .

variants.name: Liang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Jian, Feng, Wen, Wei, Ping, Ying, Cheng, Guang, Jin, Jing, Liang, Chang, Chung, Hu, Nam, Pang, Jang, Min, Neng, Shen, Shiu, Sieu, Yeong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 梁, meaning ‘roofbeam’ or ‘bridge’ in Chinese: (i) from Liang (梁), the name of a state (located in present-day Hancheng in Shaanxi Province) which was granted to Kang, the youngest son of Qinzhong (died 822  BC ), because the latter made great contributions to the fight against minority ethnic groups in western China during the reign of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After the state was annihilated by the state of Qin in 641  BC , some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from the second element of the placenames Jie Liang (解梁), Gao Liang (高粱), and Qu Liang (曲梁), names of several cities originally within the border of the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province). In 645  BC , the Duke Hui of Jin (ruler of the state of Jin, died 637  BC ) ceded these cities to the state of Qin (located originally in present-day western Shaanxi province). Later, people from these cities adopted 粱 as their surname. (iii) from Liang (梁), also known as South Liang, the name of another state (located in present-day Longxi in Gansu Province) which was granted to Tang, son of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After this state was annihilated by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from the Ba Lie Lan (拔列蘭) family, members of an ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surname to 梁 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

variants.name: Leonti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Salvatore.
Italian (Sicily): from the Greek personal name or nickname Leon, Leontos ‘lion’ (equivalent of Latin Leo, Leonis; see Leon 2).

variants.name: Lew
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wing, Yuen, Fook, Jung, Ying, Young, Yung, Bin, Foon, Hong, Kam, Koon.
Polish: from the personal name Lew ‘lion’, adopted as a translation of Leon .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Lev  .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Guyomarch II de Léon was born about 1042, in Saint-Pol-de-Léon, Finistère, Brittany, France, his father, Alain de Léon I, was 24 and his mother, Adelice du Quemenet, was 26. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Orwen de Cornouaille. He died in 1103, in Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, France, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Guyomarch II de Léon est né environ 1042, à Saint-Pol-de-Léon, Finistère, Bretagne, France, son père, Alain de Léon I, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Adelice du Quemenet, avait 26 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Orwen de Cornouaille. Il est décédé en 1103, à Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, France, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Guyomarch II de Léon um 1042 in Saint-Pol-de-Léon, Finistère, Bretagne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Alain de Léon I 24 und seine Mutter Adelice du Quemenet 26. Er hatte mit Orwen de Cornouaille mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1103 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lothringen, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Guyomarch II de Léon è nato il circa 1042 nel Saint-Pol-de-Léon, Finistère, Bretagne, Francia, il suo padre, Alain de Léon I, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Adelice du Quemenet, aveva 26 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Orwen de Cornouaille. Morì nel 1103, nel Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, Francia, all'età di 62 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Guyomarch II de Léonが約1042年にフランスブレタグンフィニストeアサイント-プオル-デ-ルeオンで生まれた時，父Alain de Léon Iは24歳，母Adelice du Quemenetは26歳であった。彼はOrwen de Cornouailleとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1103年にフランスロレィヌメウゼバー-レ-ドウックで62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Guyomarch II de Léon(이)가 대략 1042년 프랑스 브레타그니 핑니스트e르 세인트-포-데-e에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alain de Léon I(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Adelice du Quemenet(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Orwen de Cornouaille(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1103년 프랑스 로레이인 우스 바-레-드크에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Guyomarch II de Léon nasceu aproximadamente 1042, em Saint-Pol-de-Léon, Finistère, Bretagne, França, seu pai, Alain de Léon I, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Adelice du Quemenet, tinha 26 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha com Orwen de Cornouaille. Ele faleceu em 1103, em Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, França, com 62 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Guyomarch II de Léon родился около 1042 г. в местности сеинт-пол-де-лeон, финистeр, бретагн, Франция, его отцу по имени Alain de Léon I было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Adelice du Quemenet, 26. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Orwen de Cornouaille. Он умер в 1103 г. в местности бар-ле-дук, меус, лорреаин, Франция в возрасте 62 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Guyomarch II de Léon nació aproximadamente 1042, en Saint-Pol-de-Léon, Finistère, Bretagne, Francia, su papá, Alain de Léon I, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Adelice du Quemenet, tenía 26 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija con Orwen de Cornouaille. Murió en 1103, en Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, Francia, a la edad de 62 años.
